findings.title: StubLlmProvider duplicated identically across 3 test files
findings.description: The exact same 22-line StubLlmProvider class appears in all three test files (report-generator.test.ts:12-33, smart-tip.test.ts:12-33, smart-todo.test.ts:19-40). This is well beyond the 'three similar lines' threshold where extraction is justified. If the LlmProvider interface changes, all three copies must be updated in lockstep.

findings.title: makeProcessedContext and TEST_PERSONA duplicated across 3 test files
findings.description: The TEST_PERSONA constant (5 lines) and makeProcessedContext helper function (16 lines) are identically duplicated across all three test files. Combined with StubLlmProvider, each test file carries ~43 lines of shared boilerplate. The makeProcessedContext factory is a prime extraction candidate since it constructs the same default ProcessedContext shape each time.

findings.title: buildSystemPrompt first 3 lines identical across all 3 source classes
findings.description: The buildSystemPrompt() methods in ReportGenerator (line 67), SmartTip (line 83), and SmartTodo (line 133) all share the same persona preamble (first 3 template literal lines). The task-specific instructions in lines 4-7 differ. This is noted as an observation -- per the 'three similar lines' principle in CLAUDE.md, 3 shared lines is at the threshold, and the methods are short (9 lines each), so extraction may be premature. Consider extracting only if a 4th consumer appears.

findings.title: Error handling pattern (catch/onError/fallback) structurally identical in 3 classes
findings.description: All three classes (ReportGenerator:51-64, SmartTip:73-80, SmartTodo:100-107) use the same error handling pattern: wrap cause in a new Error with class-specific message, call onError if present and return a fallback value, otherwise rethrow. The pattern is structurally identical but differs in: (1) error message string, (2) fallback return value. This is acceptable duplication for now since each catch block is only 7-8 lines and the fallback values are domain-specific.
findings.suggestion:
No change recommended. The pattern is clear and each instance is short (7-8 lines). Extracting would require generic type parameters for the fallback value and a factory for the error message, adding complexity without significant clarity gain.

If this pattern spreads to more classes, consider:

async function withErrorHandler<T>(
  operation: () => Promise<T>,
  errorMsg: string,
  onError: ((e: Error) => void) | undefined,
  fallback: T,
): Promise<T> { ... }

WHY: 7-line structural repetition with domain-specific differences is within the 'three similar lines / premature abstraction' threshold when accounting for the differing fallback values.

positive_observations: All functions are short and focused: maximum function length is 23 lines (buildUserPrompt in smart-todo.ts), well within the 50-line threshold.
Cyclomatic complexity is low across all functions: maximum CC=7 in isValidSmartTipResult, all others CC<=3. No function exceeds the CC=10 concern threshold.
Maximum nesting depth is 2 levels throughout all source files -- well within the 3-level warning threshold.
Early returns and guard clauses are used consistently (e.g., dedup empty check, buildUserPrompt empty activities, isValidSmartTipResult).
Named constants VALID_KINDS and VALID_URGENCIES used instead of inline string literals in smart-tip.ts validation.
Error messages include class name context (e.g., 'ReportGenerator failed to generate daily summary'), making debugging straightforward.
cosineSimilarity is a clean pure function extracted at module scope, not a class method -- follows functional core principle.
